verses.text: Moses blesses Israel, telling of God's coming in glory. The blessing. He blesses before his death.
Moses blesses Israel, telling of God's coming in glory. The blessing. He blesses before his death.
Moses blesses Israel, telling of God's coming in glory. The blessing. He blesses before his death.
Moses blesses Israel, telling of God's coming in glory. The blessing. He blesses before his death.
Moses blesses Israel, telling of God's coming in glory. The blessing. He blesses before his death.
Reuben: let him live and not die. Reuben. A word of survival.
Judah: bring him to his people; be a help against his foes. Judah. A prayer for the royal tribe.
Levi: give him the Urim; he teaches the law and offers. Levi. The priestly tribe is blessed.
Levi: give him the Urim; he teaches the law and offers. Levi. The priestly tribe is blessed.
Levi: give him the Urim; he teaches the law and offers. Levi. The priestly tribe is blessed.
Levi: give him the Urim; he teaches the law and offers. Levi. The priestly tribe is blessed.
Benjamin: the beloved shall dwell in safety by Him. Benjamin. Safe between God's shoulders.
Joseph: blessed with the precious things of heaven and earth. Joseph. Abundant blessing on the fruitful.
Joseph: blessed with the precious things of heaven and earth. Joseph. Abundant blessing on the fruitful.
Joseph: blessed with the precious things of heaven and earth. Joseph. Abundant blessing on the fruitful.
Joseph: blessed with the precious things of heaven and earth. Joseph. Abundant blessing on the fruitful.
Joseph: blessed with the precious things of heaven and earth. Joseph. Abundant blessing on the fruitful.
Zebulun and Issachar: rejoice in your going out. Zebulun. They draw wealth from the sea.
Zebulun and Issachar: rejoice in your going out. Zebulun. They draw wealth from the sea.
Gad: he dwells as a lion, with the lawgiver's portion. Gad. A strong and settled tribe.
Gad: he dwells as a lion, with the lawgiver's portion. Gad. A strong and settled tribe.
Dan: a lion's whelp leaping from Bashan. Dan. A bold tribe.
Naphtali: satisfied with favour and full of blessing. Naphtali. Rich in God's favour.
Asher: dip his foot in oil; 'as thy days, so shall thy strength be.' Asher. Strength for every day.
Asher: dip his foot in oil; 'as thy days, so shall thy strength be.' Asher. Strength for every day.
'There is none like unto the God of Jeshurun'; the eternal God is thy refuge. The refuge. God Himself is their strength.
'There is none like unto the God of Jeshurun'; the eternal God is thy refuge. The refuge. God Himself is their strength.
